pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 90 (ground truth: expected_priority=High) and verifier.py line 43 (expected_priority='High')
setup_task.sh line 91 (ground truth: keyword=Internal Affairs); verifier.py searches for this keyword in case notes
setup_task.sh line 21: SUBJECT_EMAIL="${RAND_FIRST}.${RAND_LAST}@arkcase.com"; task.json description states internal employees have emails ending in @arkcase.com or @agency.gov

privileged_info_summary: The task is an internal affairs conflict-of-interest check workflow in ArkCase, a real open-source case management platform. The task uses credentials for user 'arkcase-admin@dev.arkcase.com' with password 'ArkCase1234!' to authenticate. When a subject is identified as an internal employee (email domain @arkcase.com), the system requires changing the case priority to 'High' and adding a note with the keyword 'Internal Affairs'. The task data is synthetically generated with random subject names from predefined lists and case details created via ArkCase REST API endpoints for each task execution.
